steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The notes assign apatite a Mohs hardness of 5, calcite a 3, and topaz an 8. A higher Mohs number means a harder mineral that can scratch minerals with a lower number. The correct choice must accurately reflect apatite's position relative to both calcite and topaz.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "In the Mohs scale of mineral hardness, topaz (8) is ranked higher than calcite (3).": does not correctly express apatite's comparative hardness relative to both other minerals. ✗
  • "Calcite, topaz, and apatite can be ordered by their ability to leave visible scratches on other minerals.": leaves out or misrepresents apatite's position in the hardness ordering. ✗
  • "Topaz has a Mohs scale number of 8, which means that it can scratch not only calcite but also apatite.": does not capture the relative hardness relationship among all three minerals. ✗
  • "Apatite has a Mohs scale number of 5, which means that it is harder than calcite (3) but softer than topaz (8).": states that apatite (5) is harder than calcite (3) but softer than topaz (8), directly matching the Mohs scale data and the scratch-ability rule. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
